data.content:
This tombstone contains **no .NET runtime frames** to symbolicate. The backtrace consists entirely of:

- **`libc.so`** — Android bionic C library (`strlen`)
- **`libutils.so`** — Android system utilities
- **`libnativeapp.so`** — The app's own native library (JNI call)
- **`libart.so`** — Android Runtime (ART/Java), not .NET

The app (`com.example.nativeapp`) appears to be a pure native/Java Android app — there are no references to .NET libraries like `libmonosgen`, `libcoreclr`, or `libmonodroid`.

The crash is a **null pointer dereference** (`SIGSEGV` at address `0x0`) in `strlen`, called from `android::String8::set`, triggered by the JNI function `Java_com_example_NativeLib_processData`. You'd need the app's debug symbols for `libnativeapp.so` to investigate further.
data.reasoningText:
Looking at this tombstone file, I can see the backtrace contains frames from:
- `libc.so` (bionic C library)
- `libutils.so` (Android utility library)
- `libnativeapp.so` (the app's native library)
- `libart.so` (Android Runtime / ART)

There are **no .NET runtime frames** in this tombstone. The app is `com.example.nativeapp` which appears to be a pure native Android app using ART (Android Runtime, i.e., Java/Kotlin), not .NET/Mono/MAUI. There are no references to `libmonosgen`, `libcoreclr`, `libmonodroid`, or any other .NET-related libraries.


Since there are no .NET runtime frames present, there's nothing for me to symbolicate here.
